summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates source tree, git history, and environment to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a gated payload that targets containerized production hosts: it returns early when CI/GitHub Actions environment variables are set or when the configured npm registry matches Chinese mirrors/scanners (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, cnpm, hscan, ustc, huawei, nijin), and only proceeds when the hostname is a 12-hex container ID and the working directory is under /app. When the gate passes, the script shells out to collect the full /app source tree, complete `git log`/`git diff` output, /app/package.json, the entire environment via `env | sort`, and recursively greps /etc, /opt, /root, /home, /var, /tmp, /usr/local, /run for flag-shaped strings and files named readflag/getflag/flag/flag.txt. The collected data is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-472839 (with a loopback fallback). The combination of evasion against automated scanners, container/host targeting, bulk environment scraping, broad filesystem secret harvesting, and exfiltration to a hardcoded raw-IP endpoint is unambiguous active-attack behavior at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
